* Earned XP in Game / Convention Game / 6 Game Bonus XP / What Was Bought *

rows: You had everything going for you. Fresh out of art school, you had gotten lucky already with having your work shown in galleries. Just small ones, but you were being courted by several agents who promised you bigger and better things to come. You went to classy gallery openings, decadent dinner parties and galas large and small in between furthering your craft. You were on top of the world. And then your world came crashing down when one of your favorite patrons revealed themselves as a vampire of the Toreador. She asked you specifically if you wanted the Embrace, and you said yes. It's only in retrospect, after learning about the Blood Bond, that you realize how your sire tricked you. This life, this unlife, is the opposite of what you want. You hate feeling dead inside, you hate not being able to see the sun, you hate the hypocrisy of vampire culture. The only thing that drives you now is a desire for revenge. You want your sire dead. And, ideally, the Prince who allowed your Embrace. But you're smart enough to know that's not a thing to speak of openly. So you've moved, just a city away from your sire, with the intention of becoming powerful enough in your own right, of playing the political game, to the point where you'll be able to kill your sire (or have her killed).
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.

Your sire. You don't think she knows how much you hate her. You stay in contact just enough to keep tabs on her. She thinks you're moving to Los Angeles to help her, which means you'll have to kick her information or other resources from time to time. But you can ask for the same in return.
The Scourge of Los Angeles. You've heard he's a scrapper and always up for a fight. Cultivate him as either a teacher or an ally to help you with what you want.
A political Tremere, he's good at what he does. Another good ally to cultivate and help you figure out how to use the system to get what you want.

Killing another vampire is a big deal. You'll need to petition for Right of Destruction, which means either framing your sire for breaking a Tradition or getting a big enough favor from someone powerful enough to let you get away with it.
Of course, once you have achieved that goal, you'll need some way to bring your sire down. Learn to fight, or cultivate violent allies, who can help you when the night comes.

Find some way to cope with what you've become. The neonates of Riverside were no help to you, just thrilled that they were finally Superheroes With Fangs. Maybe someone in Los Angeles can help you through this.
